items.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
items.raw_content: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for "high-volume work"; and Luna, a […]

items.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
items.raw_content: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of […]

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.raw_content: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
items.raw_content: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.
items.summary: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

items.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
items.raw_content: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.
items.summary: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

items.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
items.raw_content: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

items.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
items.raw_content: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]

items.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
items.raw_content: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.
items.summary: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
items.raw_content: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.
items.summary: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

items.title: Anthropic’s Mythos mess is only getting worse
items.raw_content: It's been two weeks since Anthropic took its Mythos-class models offline after a Friday evening ultimatum from the Trump administration. The company sprang into action immediately, sending a barrage of executives to Washington, DC. But updates have been suspiciously lacking, with no resolution in sight. Anthropic declined to comment multiple times this week about the […]

items.title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
items.raw_content: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.

items.title: General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
items.raw_content: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.
items.summary: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

items.title: Facebook’s Creator Studio has been revived as an AI companion app
items.raw_content: Meta is bringing back the Facebook Creator Studio page manager, now "reimagined" as a standalone AI companion app. The new app aims to make it easier for creators to connect with their audiences and show them "exactly how to grow on Facebook," according to Meta's announcement. Meta's AI Creator Assistant is a central focus of […]

items.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
items.raw_content: OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday. Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it's designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI […]

items.title: The $27 million Al proxy war over Alex Bores ends in a draw
items.raw_content: The expensive, $27 million political proxy war between Anthropic and OpenAI came to a draw last night when Alex Bores, a New York state Assemblyman whose popularity surged after being targeted by a pro-AI super PAC, narrowly lost the Democratic primary to represent New York's 12th Congressional district. items.title: Figma now has AI motion graphics and shader tools
items.raw_content: Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives "push their ideas further" and automate tedious tasks with AI. items.title: Ford had to hire back former engineers to fix mistakes made by its automated systems
items.raw_content: To celebrate its new status as No. 1 in JD Power's initial quality ranking among mainstream automakers, Ford is opening up about the challenges it has faced in recent years, especially around its reliance on automated systems in production and design. items.title: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
items.raw_content: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for "spellcheck" in an amendment summary for a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says "NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI." Luna issued the response after accounts on X began sharing screenshots of an amendment summary […]

items.title: The Google Home Speaker sounds good and looks great — but it’s finicky
items.raw_content: Right out of the box, the new Google Home Speaker passed a couple of important tests. Even with the volume at 100 percent and music blaring out of the speaker, it quickly ducked the audio and listened every time I said "Hey, Google."
